Patch Set 6:

Patch Set 6: (3 inline comments)

Yes, the changes to reply and ldlm ast request fix the interoperability problem.

If it needn't a re-submit, I'll make a follow-on patch to fix other problems. Thank you.

Patch Set 6: (11 inline comments)

So functionally this probably works, but there are a lot of bits of code that don't seem to meet with the coding standards we require and in particular the test case looks very weak to me. I'm not saying I can tell you how to write a better test case, but effectively 20 lines of bash to test 1100 lines of code that change seems incredible.

So maybe we should push this given the time constraints we are under, but I would prefer to see the tests tightened up and some of the code changed to be safer and closer to our defined standards.

Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(13 inline comments)

I saw several  relatively small defects here, so -1.  Also, this needs more and better tests.

commit.subject: LU-694 ptlrpc: Job Stats
commit.message:
LU-694 ptlrpc: Job Stats

This feature is to collect filesystem operation stats for the jobs
running on Lustre.

When some job scheduler (SLURM, for instance) is running on lustre
client, the lustre client will pack the job id into each request
(open, unlink, write...), and server will collect those information
then expose them via procfs.

- A 'pb_jobid' is added in 'ptlrpc_body' for storing the jobID;
- The job stats can be accessed from the proc file 'job_stats' in
  each mdt & obdfilter proc entries;
- 'job_cleanup_interval' is created in each mdt & obdfilter proc
  entries for tuning stats cleanup interval;
- 'jobid_var' is created in llite proc entry for configuring the
  jobID environment variable name;

Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <niu@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I35c21f93d7ce1ce648504ce437fcfd374f891453
